title: Allergic rhinitis (starter evidence summary)
text:
Allergic rhinitis commonly causes sneezing, itchy/watery eyes, itchy nose, and clear runny nose.
Fever is not typical; fever suggests infection or another cause.

Emergency red flags (ER now):
- Trouble breathing, wheezing, or swelling of lips/tongue/face (possible severe allergic reaction)

Urgent evaluation (same-day):
- Eye pain, light sensitivity, or vision changes
- High fever or severe facial pain (consider sinusitis or other infection)

Supportive care (if mild):
- Allergen avoidance, saline rinse; OTC allergy medications may help if safe (avoid dosing).
- Seek in-person care if symptoms worsen or red flags appear.
